name: Broiled Eggplant with Walnut Sauce
recipeIngredient: 1 cup Walnut meats (this season's, if possible)
recipeInstructions:
1. Put the walnuts, garlic, bread crumbs in a food processor and process briefly until everything is the texture of fine crumbs.
2. With the motor running, gradually pour in about 1 cup of boiling water. Stop and scrape down the sides, then add enough water until it reaches the desired consistency (it will thicken somewhat as it sits). Stir in the oil and fennel seeds and season to taste with salt and pepper.
3. Salt and lightly oil 4 rounds of eggplant that are about 3/8-inch to 1/2-inch thick.
4. If the eggplant is mature with lots of developed seeds when sliced, lightly salt the slices and let them sit for 30 minutes. Drain on paper towels to draw off bitter juices. Discard any liquid.
5. Preheat the broiler.
6. Brush both sides of the eggplant generously with oil.
7. Place about 3 inches from the heat source and broil until golden brown, turning once halfway through cooking.
8. Turn the rounds over and continue broiling until nicely colored and tender. If the eggplant looks dry, brush a little more oil over the top.
9. Set the broiled eggplants on a platter. Spoon walnut sauce over the tops, season with pepper, and garnish with fennel leaves or parsley. Serve hot or slightly cooled.
(If not using the sauce immediately, omit the garlic and add it just before serving.)
